conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'll be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a son, Alex. He's 18 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to know a bit more about his situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful, thank you. Could you tell me what date Alex started this approved education or training (his A-levels)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He started his A-levels in September last year, so that would be September 2024.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And when does Alex turn 19 years old?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He'll turn 19 later this year. Let me think... his birthday is in about 6 months or so.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just to confirm a few more details:

Are you the person who is primarily responsible for Alex - meaning, does he live with you and are you responsible for his day-to-day care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. He lives here at home with me and I'm responsible for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just one more question to complete my assessment:

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

- Alex is 18 years old and is in approved education (doing his A-levels at college)
- You are responsible for him and he lives with you in the UK
- For young people aged 16-19, Child Benefit can be claimed if they're in approved education or training

**Important to note:** Your Child Benefit for Alex will continue until **31st August following his 19th birthday** or until he leaves his approved education or training, whichever comes first. Since Alex will turn 19 in about 6 months, you'll be able to claim Child Benefit at least until the end of August following his 19th birthday (assuming he continues in education).

If you haven't already claimed, you should apply for Child Benefit. If you're already claiming, your benefit should continue as long as Alex remains in approved education and until the end of August after he turns 19.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and in approved education (A-levels at college), which he started in September 2024. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can be claimed for young people aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Alex will turn 19 in approximately 6 months. Child Benefit continues until the 31st August following their 19th birthday, or until they leave approved education or training, whichever comes first. Since Alex is currently under 19, lives with the claimant who is responsible for him, is in approved education, and the claimant lives in the UK, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Alex meets the age requirement (under 20), is in approved education (A-levels), and lives with the claimant in the UK who is responsible for him. Child Benefit will continue until 31st August following Alex's 19th birthday or until he leaves education, whichever comes first.
